name: Active Directory Attack Chain: Kerberoasting to Domain Takeover
description: A stealthy Active Directory attack chain modeled on real APT tradecraft: initial foothold via phishing, AS-REP Roasting for pre-auth disabled accounts, Kerberoasting to obtain service account TGS tickets, offline password cracking, Golden Ticket forging with stolen KRBTGT hash, and full domain persistence via DCSync and skeleton key injection.

phases.name: AS-REP Roasting — Pre-Authentication Disabled Accounts
phases.description: Attacker enumerates accounts with Kerberos pre-authentication disabled (DONT_REQ_PREAUTH flag). AS-REP responses are captured and the encrypted portion is extracted for offline cracking without requiring domain credentials.
phases.technique_name: Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets: AS-REP Roasting

events: EventID 4768 with RC4 encryption from unusual host

phases.name: Active Directory Enumeration — BloodHound/SharpHound
phases.description: SharpHound collector runs LDAP queries to build a complete AD graph. BloodHound analysis reveals attack paths to Domain Admins through Kerberoastable service accounts and ACL abuses.

phases.name: Kerberoasting — SPN Ticket Request
phases.description: Using a low-privileged domain account, the attacker requests TGS tickets for all accounts with Service Principal Names (SPNs). The tickets are extracted and sent offline for cracking against weak service account passwords.
phases.technique_name: Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets: Kerberoasting

events: EventID 4769 with RC4 (0x17) encryption type from non-DC host

phases.name: Privilege Escalation via Cracked Service Account
phases.description: The SQL service account password (cracked offline via hashcat) grants local admin on srv-db-01. Combined with SeImpersonatePrivilege, the attacker escalates to SYSTEM using PrintSpoofer.

phases.name: DCSync — KRBTGT Hash Extraction
phases.description: With Domain Admin equivalent rights, the attacker performs DCSync to replicate the KRBTGT account hash. This enables forging Golden Tickets for unlimited domain persistence.

phases.name: Golden Ticket Forging and Domain Persistence
phases.description: Using the KRBTGT hash, the attacker forges a Golden Ticket with a 10-year validity, granting any user domain admin access on any service. Skeleton key is also injected into the DC for backdoor access.
phases.technique_name: Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets: Golden Ticket

events: EventID 4624 Logon Type 3 with unusual SID
